calculus-volume-1:c1s1.reason: Function review in Calc 1 assumes precalculus function notation and domain/range.

calculus-volume-1:c1s2.reason: Function classes build on function review and prior polynomial/power fluency.

calculus-volume-1:c1s3.reason: Trigonometric functions assume familiarity with functions and precalc trig.

calculus-volume-1:c1s4.reason: Inverse functions require one-to-one functions and precalc inverses.

calculus-volume-1:c1s5.reason: Logarithms are defined as inverses of exponentials; precalc exp/log helps.

calculus-volume-1:c2s2.reason: Limits are defined on functions; precalc intro limits provide intuition.

calculus-volume-1:c2s3.reason: Limit laws evaluate limits defined previously.

calculus-volume-1:c2s5.reason: Epsilon-delta proofs formalize the limit concept; not required for applications.

calculus-volume-1:c3s1.reason: The derivative is defined as a limit; precalc derivative intro helps.

calculus-volume-1:c3s2.reason: Derivative-as-a-function extends the pointwise derivative definition.

calculus-volume-1:c3s3.reason: Differentiation rules operate on the derivative function.

calculus-volume-1:c3s4.reason: Applied rates interpret the derivative in context.

calculus-volume-1:c3s5.reason: Trig derivatives need trig functions and basic differentiation rules.

calculus-volume-1:c3s6.reason: The chain rule extends the power, product, and quotient rules.

calculus-volume-1:c3s7.reason: Inverse-function derivatives use inverses and the chain rule.

calculus-volume-1:c3s8.reason: Implicit differentiation applies the chain rule to implicitly defined curves.

calculus-volume-1:c3s9.reason: Exponential and logarithmic derivatives need those functions and the chain rule.

calculus-volume-1:c4s1.reason: Related rates problems require derivatives and the chain rule.

calculus-volume-1:c4s2.reason: Linear approximation uses the derivative at a point.

calculus-volume-1:c4s3.reason: Finding extrema uses the first derivative.

calculus-volume-1:c4s4.reason: Mean Value Theorem connects continuity, differentiability, and the derivative.

calculus-volume-1:c4s5.reason: Curve sketching uses first and second derivative tests built on critical points.

calculus-volume-1:c4s6.reason: Limits at infinity and asymptotes extend limit evaluation and graph analysis.

calculus-volume-1:c4s7.reason: Optimization sets up and solves extrema problems; does not require L'Hopital's Rule.

calculus-volume-1:c4s8.reason: L'Hopital's Rule evaluates indeterminate limits; requires derivatives and limits at infinity, not optimization.

calculus-volume-1:c4s9.reason: Newton's Method iterates using the derivative; independent of optimization or L'Hopital.

calculus-volume-1:c4s10.reason: Antiderivatives reverse differentiation rules, including exp/log derivatives.

calculus-volume-1:c5s1.reason: Riemann sums use limits and antiderivative intuition.

calculus-volume-1:c5s2.reason: The definite integral is defined as a limit of Riemann sums.

calculus-volume-1:c5s3.reason: The Fundamental Theorem connects differentiation and the definite integral.

calculus-volume-1:c5s4.reason: Integration formulas and net change apply FTC and antiderivatives.

calculus-volume-1:c5s5.reason: u-substitution is the chain rule in reverse.

calculus-volume-1:c5s6.reason: Integrals of exponentials and logarithms use those functions and basic integration.

calculus-volume-1:c5s7.reason: Inverse trig integrals use inverse trig functions and substitution.

calculus-volume-1:c6s1.reason: Area between curves is computed with definite integrals.

calculus-volume-1:c6s2.reason: Volume by slicing integrates cross-sectional areas.

calculus-volume-1:c6s3.reason: Shell method is an alternative volume technique; needs definite integrals.

calculus-volume-1:c6s4.reason: Arc length integrals use substitution and the chain rule.

calculus-volume-1:c6s5.reason: Work and density applications set up definite integrals.

calculus-volume-1:c6s6.reason: Centers of mass extend physical integration applications.

calculus-volume-1:c6s7.reason: Defines ln and e via integrals; builds on FTC and log/exp integration.

calculus-volume-1:c6s8.reason: Exponential growth/decay models use exponential functions and integrals.

calculus-volume-1:c6s9.reason: Hyperbolic functions are introduced with their derivatives and integrals.
